introSection: Circus Clown was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by Italian manufacturer Pinfari previously located at Chessington World of Adventures in Chessington, Greater London, England, UK.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The roller coaster was new to Smart's Amusement Park in 1987. In 1988 it was relocated to Chessington World of Adventures and called Clownaround and was located in the Circus World area. For the 1995 season, the Circus World area was removed and the coaster became part of the Toy Town area under the name Toytown Coaster. During the 2000 season, the ride was called Clown Coaster. At the end of that season it was closed. Whether the ride was sold or scrapped is not known.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride had an oval track layout.

fullText:
Circus Clown was a steel kiddie roller coaster built by Italian manufacturer Pinfari previously located at Chessington World of Adventures in Chessington, Greater London, England, UK.

The roller coaster was new to Smart's Amusement Park in 1987. In 1988 it was relocated to Chessington World of Adventures and called Clownaround and was located in the Circus World area. For the 1995 season, the Circus World area was removed and the coaster became part of the Toy Town area under the name Toytown Coaster. During the 2000 season, the ride was called Clown Coaster. At the end of that season it was closed. Whether the ride was sold or scrapped is not known.

The ride had an oval track layout.

Blue track and white supports.
